SEARCH
Module Sabre
Package edu.uky.cs.nil.sabre.prog

Interface ProgressionCost

All Known Implementing Classes:
GraphHeuristic, GraphHeuristic.AdmissibleMaxGraphHeuristic, GraphHeuristic.MaxGraphHeuristic, GraphHeuristic.SumGraphHeuristic, ReachabilityHeuristic, RelaxedPlanHeuristic, RepeatedNodeHeuristic, RepeatedRootHeuristic, WeightedCost
Functional Interface:
This is a functional interface and can therefore be used as the assignment target for a lambda expression or method reference.
@FunctionalInterface public interface ProgressionCost
A function used to measure the cost of a search node during a progression search. The progression node includes a reference to the node in the search space as well as contextual information specific to the search, such as its temporal and epistemic depth. A cost function can be used both as the cost and heuristic in a progression search, though not all cost functions will make sense in both roles.
Author:
Stephen G. Ware

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final ProgressionCost
    EXPLANATION
    A cost function that returns the explanation depth of the node it is evaluating, which is the number of actions that have been taken since the explanation this node is part of began.
    static final ProgressionCost
    PLAN_SIZE
    A cost function that returns the length of the plan that led to the state represented by the node it is evaluating.
    static final ProgressionCost
    TEMPORAL
    A cost function that returns the temporal depth of the the node it is evaluating, which is the number of actions that have been taken since the initial state.
    static final ProgressionCost
    ZERO
    A cost function that always returns 0.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    <N> double
    evaluate(ProgressionNode<N> node)
    Returns the cost of a given node based on its context in the progression search that generated it.
    default <N> void
    initialize(ProgressionNode<N> root)
    This method is called once each time a progression heuristic search is initialized with a new start state; it is used to initialize or reset this cost function before it is used for a search.

  • Field Details

    • ZERO

      static final ProgressionCost ZERO
      A cost function that always returns 0.

    • PLAN_SIZE

      static final ProgressionCost PLAN_SIZE
      A cost function that returns the length of the plan that led to the state represented by the node it is evaluating. For author nodes (epistemic depth 0), this is the number of actions that have been taken since the root of the search. For character nodes (epistemic depth > 0), this is 1 (for the root) plus the number of actions that have been taken since the root.

    • EXPLANATION

      static final ProgressionCost EXPLANATION
      A cost function that returns the explanation depth of the node it is evaluating, which is the number of actions that have been taken since the explanation this node is part of began. For nodes with epistemic depth 0 and 1 (author nodes and nodes created to explain author actions), this is the same as the plan length. For nodes with epistemic depth > 1, this number includes actions that occurred in the explanation as lower epistemic depths.

    • TEMPORAL

      static final ProgressionCost TEMPORAL
      A cost function that returns the temporal depth of the the node it is evaluating, which is the number of actions that have been taken since the initial state. For author nodes (epistemic depth 0), this is the same as the plan length. For character nodes (epistemic depth > 0), this number includes all actions that have occurred before the root of the search (even if the character did not observe them), the root, and all actions since the root.

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      default <N> void initialize(ProgressionNode<N> root)
      This method is called once each time a progression heuristic search is initialized with a new start state; it is used to initialize or reset this cost function before it is used for a search.
      Type Parameters:
      N - the type of object used to represent a node in the search's search space
      Parameters:
      root - the author's root (epistemic depth 0, temporal depth 0) that is the starting point of a progression search

    • evaluate

      <N> double evaluate(ProgressionNode<N> node)
      Returns the cost of a given node based on its context in the progression search that generated it.
      Type Parameters:
      N - the type of object used to represent a node in the search's search space
      Parameters:
      node - the search node whose cost should be evaluated
      Returns:
      the cost of the node
